title: Height Lock
description: A Minecraft mod that eliminates the annoying missclicks when you try to build a platform in the air, by allowing you to lock the Y-Level you can only build on.
body:
## This mod eliminates the annoying missclicks when building.

![Demonstration](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/5ddcc6bfcc3073ea2b1cf4219344ccc81e3817ba.gif)<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/Zzuamqc_998" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

Height Lock is a client-side lightweight Fabric utility mod that lets you lock block placement to one exact Y-level.

It does not enforce anything server-side, add a HUD overlay, or try to special-case different block types. The rule is intentionally simple: if the final placement position is not on the chosen Y-level, block it.



Compatibility:

**The ONLY requirement for this mod is the [Fabric API](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api)**

Unlock **customization features** by installing the [**Mod Menu**](https://modrinth.com/mod/modmenu) and the **[Cloth Config API](https://modrinth.com/mod/cloth-config)**. (not required)

At the moment, **no incompatibilities with other mods were found**. 

# How to use

By default, **the toggle key is H**. You can use it to toggle the mod function itself, including the grid, that marks the Y-Level.

There are three other keybinds, that are defaulted to Not Bound, which are:

- Decrease Target Y
- Increase Target Y
- Set Target Y To Current Block



Once enabled, the mod works without any need of configuration, though there are opportunities, as mentioned earlier.
